name: gsap-hero-01
title: GSAP Hero 01 — scroll parallax
description: Layout section, ships populated: full-height hero with a ScrollTrigger-scrubbed parallax background layer. First tier-2 (GSAP) item in the registry (docs/plans/motion-system.md Phase 2) — opt-in, scoped to this item only. Genuine ScrollTrigger use case (continuous scroll-linked scrub), not something the zero-dependency @astro-shad/motion runtime covers. Consumer owns nothing extra: gsap installs alongside this item only.

files.content: --- /** * GsapHero-01.astro -- layout section, tier-2 motion (docs/plans/motion-system.md * Phase 2). First GSAP-backed item in the registry: `dependencies: ["gsap@^3.13.0"]` * (caret floor, no ceiling -- GSAP 3 has shipped no breaking major release in * years, and 3.13 is where every plugin, including ScrollTrigger, became free). * * Ships populated (frontend-contract.md §7), same shape as Hero-01: zero props, * zero children renders a complete block. Content block is delimited by a * `` comment, same copy-and-own convention. * * WHY GSAP, NOT lib/motion.ts: this is a scroll-SCRUBBED parallax -- the * background layer's position is continuously tied to scroll offset, not a * one-shot entrance animation. That's explicitly tier 2's territory per the * plan (WAAPI's `IntersectionObserver`-driven triggers only fire once/toggle, * they don't scrub); ScrollTrigger's `scrub: true` is the actual justification * for pulling GSAP in at all, not decorative -- this component intentionally * does NOT reimplement anything WAAPI could already do. * * PORTING PHILOSOPHY (Phase 2, deliberately different from tier 1's WAAPI * conversions): the animation below is stock ScrollTrigger parallax code, the * same shape as GSAP's own docs/CodePen examples -- registerPlugin, a single * gsap.fromTo with a scrollTrigger config. Not funneled through motion.ts's * preset vocabulary or a custom abstraction. The goal is that a real GSAP * template found in the wild can be pasted in here with minimal translation, * not rewritten into a bespoke API. * * Architecture: bespoke per-component script, no shared tier-2 lib (mirrors * Map.astro, "first runtime npm dep" precedent) -- `gsap.registerPlugin` is * idempotent, safe to call once per component even with multiple instances * on a page. One
